clinical_history: 47-yr-old male. What is the diagnosis?  What should you do?
diagnosis: Acute anterior wall ST elevation myocardial infarction (STEMI), consistent with proximal left anterior descending (LAD) occlusion, with Q waves (V1-V3) and ST elevations (V1-V5). Stat interventional cardiology page. See most recent guidelines and updates of AHA/ACC.
